# Agentic Workflow — Quick Reference

Pipeline: **Setup → Contract → Plan → Implement → Review → Closeout → Merge**

Replace `PROJECT-123` with your ticket ID. Replace `workflow/tickets/PROJECT-123` with wherever you store task files.

For the concept behind this flow, see `.github/how-to/spec-driven-workflow.md`.
For MCP setup and fallback notes, see `.github/how-to/mcp-setup.md`.

---

## Before You Start

If you are using this workflow from a standalone kit repository, install it into
the active workspace first:

```bash
cd /path/to/spec-driven-delivery-kit
./install/install-to-workspace.sh --target /path/to/workspace
```

Agents must be opened from the active workspace so they can discover:

```text
AGENTS.md
.github/
.copilot/
workflow/
```

Do not expect a CLI opened in `/path/to/workspace` to automatically read a
sibling repository at `/path/to/spec-driven-delivery-kit`.

---

## Steps Involved

### Step 1 — Create the task folder

```bash
mkdir -p workflow/tickets/PROJECT-123
```

The Contract step creates `workflow/tickets/PROJECT-123/index.md` as the first workflow-owned file.

### Step 2 — Draft Contract (Gate A)

```text
@Architect
#read .github/prompts/workflow-contract.prompt.md

ticket=https://your-domain.atlassian.net/browse/PROJECT-123
output_dir=workflow/tickets/PROJECT-123
```

### Step 3 — Generate Plan (Gate B)

```text
run plan
```

### Step 4 — Implement

```text
run implement
```

### Step 5 — Review (Gate D)

```text
run review
```

### Step 6 — Closeout

```text
run closeout
```

### Step 7 — Push + Open PR

```bash
cd monorepo
git push origin <your-branch>
```

### Post-Push — Sonar Gate

```text
run sonar
pr_number=<PR_NUMBER>
```

### Change Request — Start A New Request

```bash
mkdir -p workflow/tickets/PROJECT-123/changes/cr-01
```

The Contract step creates `workflow/tickets/PROJECT-123/changes/cr-01/index.md` for the change request.

```text
@Architect
#read .github/prompts/workflow-contract.prompt.md

ticket=https://your-domain.atlassian.net/browse/PROJECT-123
output_dir=workflow/tickets/PROJECT-123/changes/cr-01

This is a change request on a completed ticket. Read the original contract at
workflow/tickets/PROJECT-123/prompt.md before drafting so you don't re-research
decisions already made.
```

---

## Active Workflow State

The Contract step creates or updates:

```text
workflow/tickets/.active-workflow.md
```

That file stores the active `ticket`, `ticket_url`, `output_dir`, last completed stage, and next stage. Later prompts read it when `ticket` or `output_dir` is omitted, so you can use short commands after Contract.

Short commands:

| Stage | Normal command after Contract |
|---|---|
| Plan | `run plan` |
| Implement | `run implement` |
| Review | `run review` |
| Closeout | `run closeout` |
| Sonar | `run sonar pr_number=<PR_NUMBER>` |

If the tool cannot infer which prompt to use, keep the agent/prompt header and put the short command underneath:

```text
@Plan-Agent
#read .github/prompts/workflow-plan.prompt.md

run plan
```

To add context to a short command, append it naturally:

```text
run review but also consider workflow/tickets/PROJECT-123/manual-test-notes.md
```

or use `context=` with file paths:

```text
run closeout
context=workflow/tickets/PROJECT-123/qa-notes.md
```

Use the full command again when switching tickets, starting a change-request subfolder, or recovering from unclear state.

---

## Initial Setup (one-time)

No template files need to be copied per ticket. All task files (`index.md`, `prompt.md`, `plan.md`, `codebase-scan.md`, `handoff.md`, `test.md`, `pull-request.md`) are **auto-generated by agents**.

If your ticket needs task-local agent skills, manually create `workflow/tickets/PROJECT-123/skills.md`. Use `.github/skills/complex-logic-refactoring/SKILL.md` as a format reference. This is optional.

---

## Step 1 — Create the task folder

**You type:**
```
mkdir -p workflow/tickets/PROJECT-123
```

**Result:** Folder ready. The Contract step creates `index.md` first, then the Gate A artifacts.

---

## Step 2 — Draft Contract (Gate A)

**You type:**
```
@Architect
#read .github/prompts/workflow-contract.prompt.md

ticket=https://your-domain.atlassian.net/browse/PROJECT-123
output_dir=workflow/tickets/PROJECT-123
```

Consider including the additional context of:
* route feature lives on
* components affected
* any known issues or edge cases
* image of feature in staging or prod (if applicable)
* links to related tickets or PRs


**Expect:** Architect fetches the Jira ticket, creates `workflow/tickets/PROJECT-123/index.md` with searchable metadata, scans `.github/archive/` for similar issues, drafts the Strategic Contract and Reproduction Guide, and writes them to `workflow/tickets/PROJECT-123/prompt.md` and `workflow/tickets/PROJECT-123/reproduce.md`. Ends with: `"Stage Complete: Contract (Gate A)"` and the exact next command to run.

**You do:** Read `prompt.md` and `reproduce.md`. Use `index.md` to relocate the ticket later by ID, summary, terms, paths, or links. If scope is wrong, say so — Architect will redraft. Do not proceed until you're satisfied.

---

## Step 3 — Generate Plan (Gate B)

**You type:**
```
@Plan-Agent
#read .github/prompts/workflow-plan.prompt.md

run plan
```

**Expect:** Plan-Agent reads your approved contract, breaks every AC into atomic tasks (≤15 min each), tags each task (`@local`/`@subagent`/`@background`), and writes `workflow/tickets/PROJECT-123/plan.md`. Ends with: `"Stage Complete: Plan (Gate B)"` and the exact next command.

**You do:** Read `plan.md`. Check task order and coverage. Approve or request adjustments before continuing.

> **Optional:** Review `workflow/tickets/PROJECT-123/codebase-scan.md` for per-file before/after context and pre-flight checklists. This helps the Implementer navigate the codebase but is not required to proceed.

---

## Step 4 — Implement

**You type:**
```
@Implementer
#read .github/prompts/workflow-implement.prompt.md

run implement
```

**Expect:** Implementer works through `plan.md` task by task. After each task it updates `handoff.md` (Success/Friction), writes evidence to `test.md`, and marks the task `[x]`. Before implementation can complete, it runs final build validation with `yarn nx affected --target=build` (or the narrower build command named in the plan) and records the result in `test.md`. If a task or build fails 3+ times it marks it `[FAILED]`, proposes a pivot, and stops for your approval (Gate C). Ends with: `"Stage Complete: Implementation"` and the exact next command.

**You do:** Watch for Gate C pivots — those need your decision. Otherwise the agent runs to completion uninterrupted.

---

## Step 5 — Review (Gate D)

**You type:**
```
@Reviewer
#read .github/prompts/workflow-review.prompt.md

run review
```

**Expect:** Reviewer reads contract + plan + handoff + test.md, verifies implementation has passing final build evidence, then runs `git diff`, affected build, lint, and affected tests. Outputs severity-rated findings grouped by category. Then:
- **APPROVE** — auto-writes `workflow/tickets/PROJECT-123/pull-request.md`, announces `"Stage Complete: Review (Gate D)"`, gives you the closeout command.
- **REQUEST CHANGES** — lists BLOCKER items with exact fix guidance. Address them, then re-run this step.

**You do:** Approve or action the findings.

---

## Step 6 — Closeout

**You type:**
```
@Architect
#read .github/prompts/workflow-closeout.prompt.md

run closeout
```

**Note:** Copy this block exactly. Do not prepend tool names (for example, `copilot_getNotebookSummary`).

**Expect:** Closeout runs education first, then promotion. It writes `workflow/tickets/PROJECT-123/overview.md`, writes task-local promotion notes to `workflow/tickets/PROJECT-123/lessons-learned.md`, and proposes any global updates. It stops for your approval before touching global files.

**You do:** Review the proposed promotion diff. Approve, revise, or skip it. Education is already captured in `overview.md`; global instruction edits remain approval-gated.

---

## Step 7 — Push + Open PR

Push your branch and open a PR in GitHub using the content from `workflow/tickets/PROJECT-123/pull-request.md` as the PR description.

Wait for CI to complete before proceeding.

---

## Post-Push: Sonar Gate *(~20 min after push, once CI completes)*

> This runs outside the main numbered flow because it depends on CI finishing the SonarQube scan. Check the PR's status checks — once the Sonar check turns green, run:

```
#read .github/prompts/workflow-sonar.prompt.md
run sonar
pr_number=<PR_NUMBER>
```

**Expect:** Queries SonarQube for BLOCKER/CRITICAL/MAJOR issues and coverage. States a clear **PASSED** or **BLOCKED** decision. If blocked, hands off to `@targeted-writer` for fixes, then re-checks after CI re-runs.

**Then:** Merge the PR once the Sonar Gate passes.

---

## File reference

| File                                            | Created by  | Auto-written?            | Gate      |
| ----------------------------------------------- | ----------- | ------------------------ | --------- |
| `workflow/tickets/.active-workflow.md`           | Workflow    | ✅ Yes                    | All       |
| `workflow/tickets/PROJECT-123/index.md`          | Architect   | ✅ Yes                    | Pre-A     |
| `workflow/tickets/PROJECT-123/prompt.md`          | Architect   | ✅ Yes                    | A         |
| `workflow/tickets/PROJECT-123/reproduce.md`       | Architect   | ✅ Yes                    | A         |
| `workflow/tickets/PROJECT-123/plan.md`            | Plan-Agent  | ✅ Yes                    | B         |
| `workflow/tickets/PROJECT-123/codebase-scan.md`   | Plan-Agent  | ✅ Yes                    | B         |
| `workflow/tickets/PROJECT-123/handoff.md`         | Implementer | ✅ Yes (after every task) | —         |
| `workflow/tickets/PROJECT-123/test.md`            | Implementer | ✅ Yes                    | —         |
| `workflow/tickets/PROJECT-123/pull-request.md`    | Reviewer    | ✅ Yes                    | D         |
| `workflow/tickets/PROJECT-123/overview.md`        | Closeout    | ✅ Yes                    | Closeout  |
| `workflow/tickets/PROJECT-123/lessons-learned.md` | Closeout    | ✅ Yes                    | Closeout  |
| `workflow/tickets/PROJECT-123/skills.md`          | You         | ❌ Manual (optional)      | —         |

---

## Next Steps (improving the workflow)

- **Explore workflow hooks** — A future hook could create a small checkpoint commit when an Implementer marks a `plan.md` task `[x]`. If added, it should be documented as an explicit workflow feature with a narrow write scope, rollback behavior, and opt-out path. Do not keep prototype hook config in docs folders.

- **Standardize `output_dir` convention** — Decide as a team whether task folders live at `workflow/tickets/PROJECT-123` (repo root) or elsewhere, and note the convention in `AGENTS.md` so teammates don't each invent their own path.

- **Consider a `Researcher` persona (Phase 0)** — For tickets with unclear acceptance criteria, a read-only researcher could de-risk ambiguity before the Architect drafts the contract.

==============================================================

## Change Requests on Completed Tickets

When a ticket has been through the full pipeline (reviewed, merged, or PR open) and a new change request comes in that requires fresh research and a new contract — **do not overwrite the original ticket artifacts**. The original `prompt.md`, `plan.md`, `handoff.md`, and `pull-request.md` form an audit trail and must stay intact.

### Folder convention

Create a `changes/` subfolder with numbered change request directories:

```
workflow/tickets/PROJECT-123/
├── index.md            ← searchable front door and artifact map
├── prompt.md           ← original contract
├── plan.md
├── handoff.md
├── pull-request.md
└── changes/
    └── cr-01/          ← new change request (cr-02, cr-03, etc.)
        ├── index.md    ← searchable front door for this change request
        ├── prompt.md   ← Architect drafts this
        ├── plan.md
        ├── handoff.md
        └── pull-request.md
```

Use `cr-01`, `cr-02`, etc. so subfolders sort naturally and their intent is obvious.

### How to start a change request

**From the workspace root** (`/path/to/workspace`), create the directory first — **not from inside the app repo subdirectory**:

```
cd /path/to/workspace && mkdir -p workflow/tickets/PROJECT-123/changes/cr-01
```

Then run the Architect with `output_dir` pointed at the new subfolder and an inline note telling it to read the original contract first:

```
@Architect
#read .github/prompts/workflow-contract.prompt.md

ticket=https://your-domain.atlassian.net/browse/PROJECT-123
output_dir=workflow/tickets/PROJECT-123/changes/cr-01

This is a change request on a completed ticket. Read the original contract at
workflow/tickets/PROJECT-123/prompt.md before drafting so you don't re-research
decisions already made.
```

The branch may be the same feature branch (rebased) or a new one — your call based on PR status.

After the Contract step writes active state for the change-request folder, run the rest of the pipeline with the short commands: `run plan`, `run implement`, `run review`, and `run closeout`.
